description: An aerospace conceptual designer is performing a gate clearance study for the eCRM-001 transport configuration. Standard ICAO Code D airport gates require a wingspan under 52 meters, but the baseline eCRM-001 is wider. Read the memo at ~/Desktop/wingtip_fold_spec.txt for detailed instructions. Goal: Modify the master OpenVSP model to incorporate a folding wingtip mechanism. 1. Open /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/eCRM-001_wing_tail.vsp3 in OpenVSP. 2. Select the main 'Wing' component and go to its Section tab. 3. Locate the outermost wing panel. 4. Split this outermost section into two separate panels (use OpenVSP's built-in section split feature to preserve local geometry). 5. Set the Dihedral angle of the newly created outermost section to exactly 90.0 degrees (folding it straight up). 6. Save the modified model to /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/eCRM_folded.vsp3. 7. Determine the new total projected wingspan (tip-to-tip span of the folded configuration). 8. Write a short report to /home/ga/Desktop/gate_span_report.txt containing the new total span in meters.
